name: Transandalus and Almanzora Valley Greenway Loop
name_original: Alcóntar — circular por la Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora desde El Hijate
description: This moderate 16.1-mile mountain biking loop combines sections of the Transandalus and Almanzora Valley Greenway, offering diverse scenery.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Alcóntar are situated within the Sierra de los Filabres, Almería's largest mountain range, offering diverse and challenging terrain. The area features varied topography, transitioning from lower elevations with almond and olive trees to reforested woodlands of holm oaks and pine trees at higher altitudes. Riders can expect a mix of single tracks, dry riverbeds, and gravel roads, often including technical sections and significant elevation changes. This landscape provides impressive views, sometimes extending towards the peaks of the Sierra Nevada. Best mountain bike trails around Alcóntar The most popular mountain bike trail is Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora — circular desde El Hijate, a 16.8 miles (27.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This easy route follows a greenway through the Almanzora Valley. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Transandalus and Almanzora Valley Greenway Loop, a moderate 16.1 miles (26.0 km) path. This route combines sections of the Transandalus with the greenway, offering varied terrain and scenic views. Local mountain bikers also love the Velefique climb – La Merendera Recreation Area loop from Centro Astronómico Hispano en Andalucía, a 54.0 miles (86.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant climbs, often completed in about 8 hours 3 minutes. Mountain biking around Alcóntar is defined by the dramatic hills and valleys of the Sierra de los Filabres, reforested woodlands, and dry riverbeds. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Alcóntar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 120 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alcóntar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Alcóntar?
A: There are 18 mountain bike routes around Alcóntar listed on komoot, offering a diverse range of experiences within the Sierra de los Filabres. These include 2 easy, 10 moderate, and 6 difficult trails, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking near Alcóntar?
A: The terrain around Alcóntar is highly varied, characterized by the dramatic hills and valleys of the Sierra de los Filabres. You'll encounter a mix of single tracks, dry riverbeds ('ramblas'), and gravel roads. Lower elevations feature almond and olive trees, transitioning to reforested woodlands with holm oaks and pine trees at higher altitudes. Many routes include technical sections and significant elevation changes, offering impressive views.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, there are easy options available. For a gentle ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032193372/via-verde-del-valle-del-almanzora-circular-desde-el-hijate" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora — circular desde El Hijate&lt;/a&gt;. This 16.8-mile (27.0 km) route follows a greenway, making it suitable for those looking for a less challenging experience.

Q: What are some popular circular mountain bike routes around Alcóntar?
A: Several routes offer circular options. Besides the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032193372/via-verde-del-valle-del-almanzora-circular-desde-el-hijate" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora — circular desde El Hijate&lt;/a&gt;, a popular moderate choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1142230413/transandalus-and-almanzora-valley-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transandalus and Almanzora Valley Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which combines sections of the Transandalus with the greenway for varied scenery. For a challenging long loop, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20118925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velefique climb – La Merendera Recreation Area loop from Centro Astronómico Hispano en Andalucía&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 54.0-mile (86.9 km) option.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Alcóntar?
A: The region experiences seasonal climate variations. Autumn brings vibrant colors, making it a beautiful time to ride. While winter can dust mountain roads and peaks with snow, spring and early summer generally offer pleasant conditions before the peak heat. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area offers several interesting points. You can encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4807759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serón Railway Station&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2628284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Section of the Serón Greenway&lt;/a&gt;. For impressive views, consider routes that pass through mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5306913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Venta Luisa Pass&lt;/a&gt; or offer vistas towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Baza and Los Filabres&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Alcóntar?
A: The mountain biking routes around Alcóntar are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 120 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and challenging sections available in the Sierra de los Filabres.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant climbs for advanced riders?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra de los Filabres is known for its challenging ascents. A prime example is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20118925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velefique climb – La Merendera Recreation Area loop from Centro Astronómico Hispano en Andalucía&lt;/a&gt;, a 54.0-mile (86.9 km) trail with significant elevation changes, often taking over 8 hours to complete.

Q: Can I find routes that combine different types of trails, like greenways and more rugged paths?
A: Yes, some routes offer a mix of surfaces. The moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1142230413/transandalus-and-almanzora-valley-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transandalus and Almanzora Valley Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, combining sections of the Transandalus trail with the smoother greenway, providing varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the natural features of the Sierra de los Filabres?
A: Many routes immerse you in the natural beauty of the Sierra de los Filabres. You'll find trails winding through reforested woodlands of holm oaks and pine trees, and traversing dry riverbeds. The region is also known for its natural water outcrops, which add to the picturesque scenery.

Q: What are some longer, more challenging mountain bike routes near Alcóntar?
A: For experienced riders seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20118699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serón Railway Station – El Cantaril Gorge loop from Serón&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 27.7-mile (44.5 km) route. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20118925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velefique climb – La Merendera Recreation Area loop from Centro Astronómico Hispano en Andalucía&lt;/a&gt;, which is 54.0 miles (86.9 km) long and features significant climbs.
tips:
The water from Fuente Perica comes from carbonated alpujárrides materials. Schists, phyllites and quartzites define the eastern and northern impermeable substrate and edges, while contributing to the compartmentalization of the aquifer due to the existence of numerous tectonic scales. The southern and western limits are occupied by Miocene and Plioquaternary materials, which, except for those that make up the Almanzora River alluvial, are of low permeability. The thickness of the aquiferous limestones and dolomites is estimated at 300 m.
The supply of the aquifer comes from the direct infiltration of part of the rain that falls on the permeable outcrops and surface runoff water, coming from the surrounding schists and phyllites. The discharge occurs through boreholes and through springs and galleries located on the southern edge. This group of springs emerges together with a group of small Triassic carbonate outcrops that outcrop in the Almanzora valley, in the middle of the Miocene loamy package. The waters of these springs have the peculiarity of being slightly thermal (28 °C or more), the most important being Cela, Perica, Cañada and Plaza, Algibe and Huelago. This thermalism is also manifested in certain wells, some of which are springs. Like the Cela spring, the Fuencaliente, Perica and Algibe springs are possibly linked to the existence of an important fracture, or zone of fractures forming the Fuencaliente "horst", parallel to the edge of the Almanzora depression, thanks at which the emergence of the water that precisely would have circulated in depth will take place.
Alcóntar is a small town with about 700 inhabitants. It is surrounded by beautiful mountains that are great fun for mountain biking and hiking.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring is often recommended as an ideal time to visit the Almanzora Valley Greenway section of this route. The weather is mild, and the valley bursts into color with blooming flowers. While the Transandalus offers diverse landscapes year-round, spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable riding conditions across Andalusia.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking loop?
faqs.answer: This loop combines sections of the vast Transandalus and the Almanzora Valley Greenway. The Transandalus portion can feature compact ground, trails, paths, and asphalt surfaces, traversing diverse landscapes from mountains to forests. The Almanzora Valley Greenway, a former railway line, primarily consists of compacted gravel and asphalt, making for a relatively smooth ride, though some unpaved segments might be more challenging.

faqs.question: Is this loop suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: The overall loop is rated as 'moderate' difficulty. While the Almanzora Valley Greenway section is generally suitable for all abilities, including families and children, due to its gentle descent and smooth surfaces, the Transandalus sections might require good physical condition. Some unpaved segments could be challenging or necessitate pushing your bike. It's best for those with some mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting spots. You can see the Aljibe de Serón, the Fuente Perica Spring, and the settlement of Alcóntar. The Almanzora Valley Greenway also features impressive tunnels and viaducts, remnants of its railway past, and offers views of the Sierra de los Filabres and Sierra del Lucar mountain ranges.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain biking loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends near El Hijate, close to Alcóntar. You can typically find parking in or near small villages like Alcóntar, which serves as a good access point for the trail. Always check local signage for specific parking regulations.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates significant sections of the official Transandalus trail (36% of the route) and the Vía Verde del Valle del Almanzora (25% of the route). It also uses parts of local paths such as Alcóntar - Hijate (22%) and Los Rincones (10%).

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on greenways and many natural trails in Spain, often on a leash. However, specific regulations can vary. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to carry water for them. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, typically you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to ride on the Transandalus or the Almanzora Valley Greenway. These are public access routes. However, if you plan to visit specific natural parks or attractions along the way, they might have their own entry requirements or fees.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities along the route?
faqs.answer: The Almanzora Valley Greenway connects charming towns like Arboleas, Cantoria, Olula, and Tíjola, which offer opportunities for cultural exploration, local gastronomy, and amenities. While the specific loop might not pass directly through all these towns, starting or ending in a village like Alcóntar will provide access to basic services.

faqs.question: What should I pack for mountain biking this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, essential items include sufficient water, snacks, a basic repair kit for your bike, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and appropriate clothing layers. A first-aid kit and a fully charged phone are also recommended. If you plan to explore off the main greenway, a GPS device or a downloaded map is advisable.

faqs.question: What is the elevation profile like on this loop?
faqs.answer: The loop features an elevation gain of approximately 400 meters and a similar descent. While the Almanzora Valley Greenway section is known for its gentle, almost continuous descent if ridden from Baúl to Arboleas, this specific loop combines various sections, resulting in a moderate amount of climbing and descending throughout the 26-kilometer distance.
